steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to specify how long the Gambia officially used the Gambian pound. To answer that, a choice must state both the start date (1965) and the end date (1971) — giving the full duration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Gambian pound was the Gambia's official currency from 1965 to 1971.: States the exact start and end, directly specifying the duration. ✓
  • Choice B — Though the Gambia currently uses the dalasi, the country's former official currency was introduced in 1965.: Gives only the start date, not the end. The duration is incomplete. ✗
  • Choice C — The dalasi has been the Gambia's official currency since 1971, when it replaced the Gambian pound.: Focuses on the dalasi's adoption, not the pound's duration. The start of the pound (1965) is absent. ✗
  • Choice D — Countries sometimes adopt new currency systems, as the Gambia did when it replaced the Gambian pound with the dalasi.: No dates at all. Cannot specify a duration. ✗
That matches Choice A — The Gambian pound was the Gambia's official currency from 1965 to 1971..
